message: chore(beads): close d5yeb after agent-mail recheck

@@ -1181,7 +1181,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d3z","title":"fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research","description":"# fcp.reddit: Authentic User Opinions Research (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide a research-focused macro surface that helps agents extract **authentic user opinions** from Reddit in a structured, bounded way.

This is not “LLM analysis inside the connector”; it is data acquisition + packaging so agents can do downstream reasoning safely.

## Scope
### Research macros (compositions)
- `reddit.research.search_threads`:
  - input: query + subreddit scope + time window
  - output: ranked thread summaries (ids, titles, scores, timestamps)
- `reddit.research.collect_evidence`:
  - input: thread ids
  - output: bounded comment excerpts with provenance pointers (post/comment ids)

### Safety/bounds
- Explicit caps:
  - max threads
  - max comments per thread
  - max total bytes returned
- NSFW/quarantine policy must be explicit.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.read`
- `reddit.search`
- `reddit.research` (macro operations)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- All content is tainted.
- Structured logs include only ids + counts.

## Deliverables
- Canonical output types:
  - `ResearchThreadSummary`
  - `ResearchEvidenceBundle`
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.research.search_threads`
  - `reddit.research.collect_evidence`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - ranking determinism
  - bounds enforcement
  - NSFW/quarantine gating

## Acceptance criteria
- Research macros are deterministic, bounded, and mechanically authorized.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d4j2","title":"[IDEA-07.3] Formatting fallback tests + example wiring","description":"## Goal
Provide tests and example wiring in a connector send_message path.

## Details
- Add integration test with a fake API returning parse errors.
- Example wiring notes in connector template or docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Example shows how to hook formatter and error classifier together.
- Tests confirm fallback behavior without leaking content in logs.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","title":"[tooling] MCP Agent Mail storage.sqlite3 corruption blocks registration and inbox access","description":"Agent Mail use in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors is currently blocked before registration or inbox access. MCP tool calls such as macro_start_session, list_agents, ensure_project, and resource reads return DATABASE_ERROR with detail: refusing automatic sqlite recovery for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/storage.sqlite3 while live WAL/SHM sidecars are present; stop the server and run explicit repair. Direct sqlite integrity_check confirms main DB corruption (freelist corruption, duplicate page references, out-of-order rowid, and many never-used pages). Current on-disk state includes storage.sqlite3, storage.sqlite3-wal, storage.sqlite3-shm, and storage.sqlite3.bak. This prevents the user-requested Agent Mail registration, inbox processing, and introductions to other agents. A bounded timeout 8 am mail inbox probe also exited 124 before the unbounded call eventually returned, so inbox access is still too slow and unreliable. Evidence collected: read_mcp_resource on resource://projects returns error_detail saying automatic sqlite recovery is refused while WAL/SHM are live; sqlite3 PRAGMA integrity_check reports freelist corruption, duplicate page references, and rowid/order damage; ls shows storage.sqlite3 plus storage.sqlite3-wal and storage.sqlite3-shm; lsof shows Agent Mail listener processes on port 8765 including /Users/jemanuel/.local/bin/am serve-http --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8765 --no-tui. sqlite3 .recover emits a plausible SQL dump, so an explicit stop-and-recover procedure looks possible, but I am not running it without user approval because it would rewrite coordination state.","created_at":"2026-03-27T17:56:13Z"},{"id":2119,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyForest attempted to switch coordination back to Agent Mail after partial recovery. A second direct read path also fails during reconciliation: `am agents list --project /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors --json` => `reconstruct: insert agent BrownLake: Query error: disk I/O error`. Non-destructive diagnostics: `am doctor check --verbose` reports FK/parity failures (8591 orphaned message_recipients, archive projects 68 vs db 67, messages 9789 vs db 7900) and high CPU on the Agent Mail listener; `am doctor repair --dry-run` reports only orphaned message recipients/inbox_stats rebuild and does not indicate a parity/archive fix; `am doctor archive-scan --json` reports 60 missing project.json files across the shared archive, including /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/projects/users-jemanuel-projects-agent-flywheel-app/project.json and a legacy flywheel_connectors slug variant. Net: the current blocker is shared archive/DB reconciliation drift affecting unrelated projects, so I could not safely register, fetch inbox, introduce myself, or reserve files without broader Agent Mail repair work.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:51:06Z"},{"id":2154,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"Codex","text":"2026-03-31 OrangeMarsh/Codex session symptom update. Agent Mail recovered enough for ensure_project, register_agent, list_agents (at least intermittently), and fetch_inbox on /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors; OrangeMarsh registration succeeded in this session. Write-path coordination is still degraded: send_message and macro_contact_handshake continue to return Resource is temporarily busy, and I could not reliably complete introductions to the other agents or reserve files through mail. This is better than total registration failure but still not enough to satisfy the repo workflow without a Beads fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:55:14Z"},{"id":2254,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverBasin","text":"2026-04-01 SilverBasin coordination/infrastructure update. I was able to temporarily repair the shared Agent Mail store enough to satisfy part of the user-requested flow: after `am doctor reconstruct -y --json`, MCP operations for ensure_project, register_agent, list_agents, fetch_inbox, and send_message worked again in /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors. I registered as SilverBasin, discovered active agents including WildTern / CalmCondor / SwiftRaven, and sent introduction messages; WildTern and CalmCondor both replied. The repair is not durable. A file-reservation smoke test created one reservation successfully, but release_file_reservations then failed with database-corruption/retry messaging and subsequent Agent Mail operations regressed again. Current session symptom is worse: MCP transport to http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/ is now failing entirely (client error sending request), so I can no longer fetch/ack inbox or use reservations. Net: reconstruct can temporarily restore identity/inbox/message flows, but reservation lifecycle still appears to re-trigger corruption and the shared server can collapse back to transport failure afterward. I am mirroring progress into beads because Agent Mail is not trustworthy enough to be the sole coordination channel.","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:49:38Z"},{"id":2259,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"SilverBasin","text":"2026-04-01 late-session Agent Mail state changed again. MCP health_check is green now, but it is serving from a salvage DB path: /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/storage.sqlite3.salvage-20260401_203739_484.sqlite3. In that state fetch_inbox for SilverBasin returns an empty list and list_agents for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors returns [], even though whois(SilverBasin) still resolves my registered profile. This looks like a partial salvage boot rather than a trustworthy full restoration of project coordination state. I therefore treated the mailbox as readable-but-not-authoritative and continued mirroring work claims/progress in beads.","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:57:02Z"},{"id":2374,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"IvoryHarbor","text":"2026-04-03 IvoryHarbor symptom update while following the explicit coordination workflow. Current Agent Mail state is unstable rather than simply down: `register_agent`, `list_agents`, `file_reservation_paths`, and at least one `send_message` succeeded for /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors, so partial write-path recovery exists. `fetch_inbox` is still flaky and alternates between success (`[]`), `A database error occurred. This may be a transient issue - try again.`, and `Resource is temporarily busy. Wait a moment and try again.` When I attempted `macro_contact_handshake` for active agents, the server returned a more specific corruption diagnosis: `Database corruption detected: cannot recover /Users/jemanuel/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo/storage.sqlite3 while live WAL/SHM sidecars are present; automatic checkpoint failed: Query error: database disk image is malformed; stop the server and run explicit repair. Run am doctor repair or am doctor reconstruct to recover.` Net: identity/send/reservation flows can appear to work transiently, but inbox/contact coordination is still not trustworthy, and any real repair would require stopping the live shared Agent Mail server first.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:29:46Z"},{"id":2375,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"IvoryHarbor","text":"Additional 2026-04-03 regression note from the same session: after successful `register_agent` as `IvoryHarbor` and at least one successful `send_message`, a later `fetch_inbox` call regressed to `Agent IvoryHarbor not found ... Available agents: CreamMountain`. This means the recovered identity row itself is not durable across requests; the mailbox can appear healthy enough to register and then lose the agent immediately afterward.","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:33:15Z"},{"id":2871,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"progress: 2026-04-26 pane-5 recheck. Prior pass showed partial direct-CLI recovery (status/list/register/inbox), but MCP transport to http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/ was still failing. Re-running live server and inbox checks now to decide close-vs-reopen with current evidence.","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:23:25Z"},{"id":2872,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d5yeb","author":"jemanuel","text":"progress: 2026-04-26 recheck outcome. am --version returned am 0.2.38, but curl http://127.0.0.1:8765/health failed immediately with connection refused, so the Agent Mail HTTP listener is currently down. Direct CLI status still works (project users-jemanuel-projects-flywheel-connectors, Messages 2116, Agents 394), but timeout 15 am mail inbox --project /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors --agent QuietLeopard --json exited 124 with no output. Net: server still broken and inbox path still unreliable; bead remains open.","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:24:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6d","title":"fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection","description":"# fcp.sentry: Event details and stack trace inspection (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable deep triage by fetching **event-level details** for an issue, including stack traces and relevant context, while maintaining strict secret/PII hygiene.

## Scope
### Read operations
- Fetch latest event for an issue (or a specific event id).
- Retrieve:
  - stack trace frames (module/function/line)
  - breadcrumbs
  - tags
  - environment + release
  - request/user context (PII: must be handled carefully)
- Optional: retrieve attachments/artifacts metadata (download is separate + bounded).

### Output shaping
- Return a **sanitized, structured** view that is useful for debugging:
  - include stable identifiers
  - include stack traces (code locations are ok)
  - redact known secret-like fields

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.events.read`
- `sentry.artifacts.read` (if attachments are exposed)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Event payloads are untrusted and may contain secrets/PII.
  - Do not log raw payloads.
  - Apply redaction before returning “rendered” textual summaries.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.event.get`
  - `sentry.event.latest_for_issue`
  - `sentry.event.stacktrace`
- Redaction strategy:
  - allowlist safe fields
  - redact common secret patterns
  - cap maximum returned payload size

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - stack trace parsing and normalization
  - redaction correctness (golden vectors)
  - size limits + early abort
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Returns are useful for debugging without leaking secrets/PII.
- Logs remain metadata-only with correlation IDs.

Also has sentry.get_transaction for performance events with spans/measurements. 169 total tests. Closing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:19:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d6xr4","title":"[FCP3/P1.1b] Inventory zone, capability, provenance, and admission leakage","description":"Background: policy leakage is a distinct problem from execution leakage. Zone binding, capability scoping, provenance, and admission semantics often appear shared only because they are smeared across broad buckets and host orchestration code.

Scope: inventory where zone rules, capability semantics, provenance constraints, admission decisions, and placement eligibility inputs are actually defined and consumed today. Identify duplicate or conflicting policy meaning across shared crates, host code, mesh code, and operator explanation paths.

Acceptance: there is a self contained policy leakage inventory that can feed the canonical owner map and later policy carve out work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:37.452083Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58.647517Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58.647172Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","inventory","mesh-first","phase-1","policy","security"],"comments":[{"id":2060,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-d6xr4","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass 2026-03-27: Covered by docs/FCP3_Semantic_Ownership_Inventory.md. Policy leakage inventory: fcp-core owns 28 policy types including PolicyEngine, CapabilityToken/Verifier, ZoneTransportPolicy, UsageBudgetPolicy. fcp-host enforcement.rs defines 11-stage pipeline (enforcement ORDER is host-local but should be platform-canonical). fwc policy_cmd.rs directly manipulates PolicyBundle + signs with Ed25519 (should go through fcp-host RPC). zone_scope.rs does MCP scoping. Forbidden overlaps: enforcement pipeline ordering, readiness model duplication, credential storage.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:23:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-d7dd","title":"[IDEA-22.2] Unit tests for posture verification","description":"## Coverage
- Valid attestation passes.
- Invalid signature fails.
- Missing posture for required zone → deny.

## Logging
- Include posture_id + zone_id in structured logs.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).
","notes":"Claimed by IndigoCanyon - adding comprehensive unit tests for posture verification

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.
